meta.title: NEPC — seizure recurrence in paediatric neurocysticercosis
meta.authors: Panda PK, Elwadhi A, Gupta D, et al.
meta.outcome: Risk of seizure recurrence over 6 months in children with parenchymal neurocysticercosis on antiseizure monotherapy, from 4 factors (NEPC score)
meta.cff_info:
What this shows. The NEPC score (Panda et al., Epilepsy Research 2023) estimates the 6-month risk of seizure recurrence in children with parenchymal neurocysticercosis on antiseizure monotherapy. Single-centre, 161 children (overall recurrence 14%; AUC 0.89).

How to read it

  • Score 0–4 (each factor 1 point). Observed recurrence: 0–1 → 0%, 2 → 45%, 3 → 53%, 4 → 85%. Optimal high-risk threshold: score ≥4.

Single-centre, internally validated, 6-month horizon, ages 4–18. Small per-score numbers (0% reflects 0 events in the low-score groups). Demonstration tool.

predictors.name: >5 NCC lesions (calcified / viable parenchymal)
predictors.assess: More than 5 neurocysticercosis lesions on imaging. 1 point.

predictors.name: Perilesional edema >2 cm (maximum dimension)
predictors.assess: Perilesional oedema exceeding 2 cm in maximum dimension. 1 point.

predictors.name: Cluster of seizures before presentation
predictors.assess: More than 2 convulsive seizures within 24 hours before presentation. 1 point.

model.note: Observed 6-month recurrence by score (single centre, n=161). Scores 0–1 had 0 recurrences (0/85 and 0/34). Optimal high-risk cut-off ≥4.

provenance.source: Panda 2023 Epilepsy Research Table 5 (points) + Table 6 (score→recurrence) — doi 10.1016/j.eplepsyres.2023.107239, PMID 37862917
provenance.notes: NEPC 0-4, 4 items each +1 (>5 lesions, edema>2cm, epileptiform EEG, seizure cluster). Observed recurrence 0=0,1=0,2=45,3=53,4=85%. AUC 0.891. Small single-centre. Built 2026-07-08.
